src-spotlight-pa-mailballot.name: Spotlight PA — Mail Ballot Date Rule Coverage
src-spotlight-pa-mailballot.notes: Tracks ongoing PA mail ballot envelope-dating litigation (state + federal).

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Last day to register to vote in the PA May 19 primary.
ELECTION_EVENTS.authority: PA Department of State / County Election Offices

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Applications must reach the county election office by 5:00 PM.
Mail-in / absentee ballots must be received by the county election office by 8 PM. Postmarks do not count.
Pennsylvania statewide primary. Polls open 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM.
ELECTION_EVENTS.notes: Bresnahan (R) won uncontested; Cognetti (D) won uncontested.

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Last day to register to vote before the November 3 general election.
ELECTION_EVENTS.authority: PA Department of State / County Election Offices

ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: Applications must reach the county election office by 5:00 PM (general election).
Mail-in / absentee ballots must be received by the county election office by 8 PM. Postmarks do not count.
U.S. midterm general election. Polls open 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM.

ELECTION_EVENTS.milestone: Mail ballot date rule — court cases ongoing
ELECTION_EVENTS.significance: 3rd Circuit ruled envelope dating requirement unconstitutional (Aug 2025). RNC + Commonwealth petitioned SCOTUS. Decision could change rules before Nov 3 general election.
ELECTION_EVENTS.notes: SCOTUS petition responses due June 1, 2026. Monitor for cert grant.

RULES.currentRule: Online, mail, or in-person registration through PA Voter Services or a county election office. Must be a U.S. citizen, PA resident in the district at least 30 days, and 18+ by election day.
RULES.whyItMatters: Determines who can cast a ballot in PA-08.
RULES.detail: Registration applications must be received at least 15 days before the election. General deadline Oct. 19, 2026.

RULES.currentRule: Any registered Pennsylvania voter may apply for a no-excuse mail-in ballot. No reason required.
RULES.whyItMatters: Mail-in ballots are a primary voting channel for many PA-08 voters.

RULES.currentRule: Absentee ballots are limited to voters who meet a qualifying reason (illness, disability, absence from municipality, etc.). Voters who don't need a reason should use a mail-in ballot.
RULES.whyItMatters: Distinguishes the two PA mail-voting tracks and which application a voter should use.

RULES.currentRule: Mail-in and absentee ballot applications must be received by the county election office by 5:00 PM seven days before election day.
RULES.whyItMatters: Late applications cannot be processed; voters must use in-person voting if missed.
RULES.detail: General: Oct. 27, 2026 at 5:00 PM. Applies to Lackawanna, Luzerne, Monroe, Pike, and Wayne county election offices.

RULES.currentRule: Polls are open 7:00 AM to 8:00 PM on election day across Pennsylvania. Any voter in line by 8:00 PM may cast a ballot.
RULES.whyItMatters: Defines the window for in-person voting in PA-08.

RULES.currentRule: First-time voters at a polling place, and voters voting at a new polling place for the first time, must show acceptable ID. Returning voters at their established polling place generally do not need ID.
RULES.whyItMatters: Affects in-person eligibility, especially for newly registered or recently moved voters in Lackawanna, Luzerne, Monroe, Pike, and Wayne counties.
RULES.detail: Confirm exact acceptable-ID list and first-time-voter rule against PA Title 25 and PA Department of State guidance before public release.

RULES.currentRule: Voters can track their mail-in or absentee ballot status through PA Voter Services from application through receipt at the county election office.
RULES.whyItMatters: Lets voters confirm their ballot was received in time and resolve issues before deadlines.

RULES.currentRule: Pennsylvania elections are administered at the county level. Mail-in and absentee applications, ballots, and in-person logistics flow through each county's election office.
RULES.whyItMatters: PA-08 voters interact with Lackawanna, Luzerne, Monroe, Pike, or Wayne county election offices depending on residence.

RULES.currentRule: Mail ballots must be physically RECEIVED by the county election office by 8:00 PM on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.
RULES.whyItMatters: The most common cause of ballot rejection. Ballots arriving after 8 PM on November 3 will not be counted, even if postmarked earlier.
RULES.detail: PA Election Code § 1306-D. Receipt deadline: November 3, 2026 at 8:00 PM. Apply well before the Oct 27 request deadline.

RULES.currentRule: Pennsylvania does not offer in-person early voting at polling places. Pre-Election Day voting requires requesting and returning a mail-in or absentee ballot.
RULES.whyItMatters: Voters who want to vote early must use the mail-in/absentee process and meet the Oct 27 application deadline.

RULES.currentRule: Only registered Democrats and Republicans may vote for candidates in their party's primary. Independents and third-party registrants cannot vote in candidate primaries (though they may vote on ballot questions).
RULES.whyItMatters: Unaffiliated voters in Lackawanna, Luzerne, Monroe, Pike, and Wayne counties cannot participate in candidate selections for the primary.
RULES.detail: PA Election Code § 25-2862. Voters can change party registration; deadline to change for general primary is 15 days before the election.

RULES.currentRule: Counties currently cannot reject mail ballots for undated or misdated outer envelopes per 3rd Circuit ruling (Aug 2025). SCOTUS petition pending (Commonwealth v. Eakin). Responses due June 1, 2026.
RULES.whyItMatters: A SCOTUS reversal could invalidate ballots with undated envelopes in the November 3 general. Voters should always date their return envelope to protect against a rule change.
RULES.detail: Commonwealth v. Eakin, No. 25-967 at SCOTUS. Monitor for cert grant or denial after June 1 response deadline.

RULES.topic: County notice of mail ballot errors
RULES.currentRule: Per PA Supreme Court (Coalfield Justice, 2024), county boards must notify voters of disqualifying errors on return envelopes before rejecting a ballot, giving the voter a chance to cast a provisional ballot.
RULES.whyItMatters: Voters with envelope errors may have a remedy if the county notifies them in time to vote provisionally on Election Day.
RULES.detail: Center for Coalfield Justice v. Washington County Board of Elections (PA Sup. Ct. 2024). Applies across all PA counties including Lackawanna, Luzerne, Monroe, Pike, and Wayne.

UPDATES.title: PA-08 primary held May 19 — Bresnahan (R) and Cognetti (D) advance to November general
UPDATES.newValue: Rob Bresnahan Jr. (R) won uncontested (37,937 votes); Paige Cognetti (D, Mayor of Scranton) won uncontested (58,374 votes). Both advance to Nov. 3, 2026 general.

UPDATES.title: SCOTUS petition pending — Commonwealth v. Eakin mail ballot dating case
UPDATES.oldValue: 3rd Circuit ruling (Aug 2025): counties cannot reject undated/misdated ballots
UPDATES.newValue: RNC and Commonwealth petitioned SCOTUS in Feb 2026. Responses due June 1, 2026. Outcome uncertain for Nov 3 general.

UPDATES.title: PA-08 dashboard initialized — all general election deadlines configured
UPDATES.newValue: All 2026 election events, sources (14), rules, and resources configured for initial release.

UPDATES.title: General-election deadlines re-verified against PA Department of State; no changes detected
UPDATES.oldValue: Deadlines confirmed as of 2026-05-26 initial build.
UPDATES.newValue: As of the June 17, 2026 refresh, PA Department of State guidance still lists October 19, 2026 as the voter registration deadline, October 27, 2026 as the mail-in/absentee application deadline, and November 3, 2026 as Election Day with mail ballots due by 8:00 PM. Postmarks do not count. Envelope-dating signature requirement still in effect per PA DoS mail-ballot guidance.

UPDATES.title: SCOTUS denied RNC emergency request on PA provisional-ballot backstop (June 6, 2026)
UPDATES.oldValue: Provisional-ballot backstop under PA Supreme Court ruling in Center for Coalfield Justice v. Washington County Board of Elections still operative; awaiting any federal challenge.
UPDATES.newValue: On June 6, 2026, the U.S. Supreme Court denied the RNC's emergency request to block PA from counting provisional ballots cast by voters whose mail-in ballots were invalid due to envelope errors. Denial was procedural — the case (limited to two voters in one county) was not a proper vehicle for statewide relief. Justices Alito, Thomas, and Gorsuch wrote separately leaving the underlying constitutional question open. The Coalfield Justice provisional-ballot backstop remains operative for November 3, 2026.

CONFLICTS.title: Mail ballot envelope dating — conflicting court rulings (state + federal)
CONFLICTS.description: 3rd Circuit: date requirement unconstitutional (Aug 2025). PA Commonwealth Court: same (2024). PA Supreme Court: counties must notify voters of errors (Coalfield Justice, 2024). RNC + Commonwealth petitioned SCOTUS (Feb 2026). If SCOTUS reverses, ballots without accurate dates could be rejected in the Nov 3, 2026 general election.

NEWS.headline: SCOTUS responses due June 1 in PA mail-ballot dating case — ruling could affect November
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Responses in Commonwealth v. Eakin (No. 25-967) were due at SCOTUS on June 1, 2026. If the Court takes the case and reverses the 3rd Circuit, counties could again reject undated PA-08 mail ballots before the November 3 general election.

NEWS.headline: Paige Cognetti wins Democratic primary for PA-08 — Scranton mayor to face Bresnahan in November
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Mayor of Scranton Paige Cognetti won the Democratic primary uncontested (58,374 votes). She will face incumbent Rep. Rob Bresnahan Jr. (R) in the November 3 general election. Race rated R+4 Cook PVI; Democrats targeting as a pickup opportunity.

NEWS.headline: Enforcing date requirement for PA mail ballots is ruled unconstitutional (3rd Circuit)
NEWS.relevanceSummary: 3rd Circuit ruled unanimously that PA cannot reject mail ballots for undated or misdated outer envelopes. Eakin v. Adams County Board of Elections. This directly affects PA-08 mail voters.

NEWS.headline: RNC asks U.S. Supreme Court to take up Pennsylvania mail ballot dating case
NEWS.relevanceSummary: RNC and Commonwealth of Pennsylvania both petitioned SCOTUS (Commonwealth v. Eakin, No. 25-967). Responses due June 1, 2026. A ruling could change PA mail ballot rules before the November 3 general election.

NEWS.headline: PA Supreme Court rules counties must alert voters whose mail ballots contain errors
NEWS.relevanceSummary: PA Supreme Court affirmed in Coalfield Justice v. Washington County that county boards must notify voters of disqualifying errors on mail ballot envelopes, giving them a chance to cast a provisional ballot.

NEWS.headline: Automatic voter registration in PA has enrolled over 350,000 voters — PA-08 counties among top gainers
NEWS.relevanceSummary: PA's AVR program registered 353,275 new voters. Pike County (within PA-08) saw outsized gains — 27% of eligible-unregistered residents newly enrolled. However AVR registrants vote at lower rates (32.6%) than traditional new registrants.

NEWS.headline: PA mail ballot date rule sparks years of court battles — PA Supreme Court weighing in
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Comprehensive timeline of PA mail ballot dating litigation since Act 77 (2019). State and federal courts have repeatedly conflicted. Baxter v. Schmidt (PA Supreme Court, Sep 2025) and Commonwealth v. Eakin (SCOTUS, 2026) are the live cases to watch.

NEWS.headline: PA primary results: Cognetti (D) to face Bresnahan (R) in PA-08 lean-R rematch territory
NEWS.relevanceSummary: Statewide primary recap: Scranton Mayor Paige Cognetti ran unopposed for the Democratic nomination; incumbent Rob Bresnahan (R) ran unopposed in the Republican primary. Sabato's Crystal Ball rates the Nov. 3 race lean Republican.

NEWS.headline: SCOTUS denies RNC emergency request to block PA provisional-ballot backstop
NEWS.relevanceSummary: On June 6, 2026, SCOTUS denied the RNC's emergency request to stop PA from counting provisional ballots from voters whose mail-in ballots had envelope errors. Denial was procedural (case was limited to two voters in one county). Alito/Thomas/Gorsuch wrote separately leaving the constitutional question open.

LAST_REFRESH.summary: June 17, 2026 bi-weekly refresh for PA-08. Re-verified PA Department of State upcoming-elections and mail-in/absentee guidance directly: general-election deadlines unchanged (Oct. 19 registration, Oct. 27 mail-application, Nov. 3 Election Day and 8:00 PM mail-ballot receipt; postmarks do not count; envelope-dating signature requirement still in effect). Cognetti (D, unopposed) vs. Bresnahan (R, incumbent, unopposed) confirmed for Nov. 3. New: documented June 6, 2026 SCOTUS denial of RNC emergency request on PA provisional-ballot backstop (Coalfield Justice rule survives). Commonwealth v. Eakin (envelope-dating) still pending at SCOTUS. Watson v. RNC (Mississippi/Alaska late ballots) decision expected end of June; PA receipt-by-8pm rule unaffected directly. All lastVerified dates bumped to 2026-06-17. No confidence flips.

WEEKLY_REPORT.summary: Bi-weekly period June 3–17, 2026: General-election deadlines re-verified directly against PA DoS — unchanged. Cognetti (D) vs. Bresnahan (R) confirmed for Nov. 3 (Sabato rates lean R). New litigation watch: SCOTUS denied RNC emergency request (June 6) on PA provisional-ballot backstop — Coalfield Justice rule survives. Commonwealth v. Eakin (envelope-dating) still pending. No procedure changes detected; no confidence flips.
